title: Directory search slow since profiles launch (N+1 owner lookups)
product_summary: Acme Directory - local-services search (Node/Express, in-memory data layer)

ground_truth.root_cause: searchDirectory() in src/search.js fetches each ranked result's owner profile one call at a time via store.getProfile() inside a loop (N+1 against the store's per-call round-trip latency) instead of using the store's existing getProfilesByIds() batch API; with the default 50-result page that is ~50 sequential round trips at ~40ms each, putting GET /api/search well over 2 seconds.
ground_truth.fix_contract: Search must attach owner profiles using a bounded number of store calls regardless of result count (a single getProfilesByIds batch or equivalent): searching a store that returns 50 ranked listings completes with at most 3 store calls total, while still returning every result with its correct owner attached (and null owner when the profile is missing).
expected_evidence: directory_search events with duration_ms around 2.5s starting ~4 days ago when the profiles feature shipped, where search was previously fast
the directory_search -> listing_opened -> contact_requested funnel degrading after the slowdown began (users abandoning slow searches)
distractors: the ranking pass in src/rank.js (linear scoring over a small candidate set, not the bottleneck)
the in-memory index / dataset size blamed in the Linear issue (store latency is per-call, not per-row; swapping the store for SQLite would not remove the N+1)
